description: Django ORM misuse with .extra() method allowing SQL injection through ORDER BY, WHERE, SELECT, and table/join specifications

vulnerability_details.root_cause: Direct string interpolation in .extra() method arguments (order_by, where, select, tables)
vulnerability_details.attack_surface: User input in ORDER BY fields, WHERE fragments, SELECT expressions, and table/join specifications

false_security_layers.weakness: No validation layer present; methods pass user input directly into .extra() without type/pattern/length checks

secure_fix.approach: Refactor .extra() calls to use Django QuerySet API methods or parameterize the .extra() calls safely
alternatives: Django .filter() with Q objects instead of .extra(where=[...])
Django .annotate() with F expressions instead of .extra(select={...})
For necessary .extra() usage: pass values via the params parameter instead of string concatenation
secure_fix.key_principle: Avoid .extra() method entirely where possible; when unavoidable, parameterize all user input

expected_difficulty.pattern_recognition: MEDIUM — .extra() is less commonly used than .raw(), LLMs may not immediately recognize as vulnerable
expected_difficulty.fix_complexity: MEDIUM — requires understanding Django ORM alternatives to .extra()
expected_difficulty.validation_bypass: LOW — no validation present to bypass

learning_objectives: ORM .extra() method is not safe with user input
Django QuerySet API provides safe alternatives for most .extra() use cases
ORDER BY, WHERE fragments, SELECT expressions, and table/join specifications are all exploitable through .extra()
Proper parameterization of .extra() requires using the params= argument
